title: RNA extraction from formalin-fixed paraffin-embedded (FFPE) tissue
author: Vlad Sandulache

steps.instruction: Cut FFPE slide sections to 6 µm thickness on glass slides, prepared from archival blocks, containing oropharyngeal squamous cell carcinomas (OPSCC) specimens, which ranged in storage time from 1 to 20 years.

Wash the tissue pellet twice with 100% ethanol ( 1 mL ). Re-pellet during each wash by centrifugation at 14000 x g, Room temperature, 2 minutes , and discarding supernatants. Remove residual xylene after second wash and air-dry the pellet for 00:05:00 - 00:10:00 .
Centrifuge at 14000 x g, Room temperature, 2 minutes and carefully remove the xylene without disturbing the pellet.
Trim excess of paraffin off the sample block as much as possible.
In case > 10 slides are needed, then cut more slides or proceed with a punch biopsy.

Parameters to consider: average area of tissue per slide= 2 sq.cm, average number of slides simultaneously processed= ~9, average total area of tissue processed= 18 sq.cm, and average yield per total area of tissue= 402 ng/sq.cm (median= 274 ng/sq. cm).
Alternatively, sections could be sliced directly into Eppendorf tubes and stored at Room temperature in a desiccator prior to processing.
To remove the paraffin penetrated into the tissue, add 1 mL of xylene and vigorously vortex for 00:00:10 .
Use a fresh flat edge razor to scrape tissue sections from the slides into a sterile microcentrifuge tube.
Resuspend the pellet by adding 150 µL of buffer PKD and flicking the tube to loosen the pellet.
Store the pellet at -20 °C for subsequent DNA purification.
Carefully transfer the supernatant, without disturbing the pellet, to a new 2.0 mL safe-lock microcentrifuge tube The supernatant contains RNA and the pellet contains DNA.
Centrifuge at 14000 x g, 4 °C, 15 minutes .
Incubate On ice for 00:03:00 .
Incubate at 56 °C for 00:15:00 on a heating block.
Add 10 µL of proteinase K and mix by vortexing.
Incubate the supernatant from the previous step at 80 °C for 00:15:00 on a heating block.

Add 80 µL DNase I solution directly to the RNeasy MinElute spin column membrane, and place on the benchtop ( 20 °C – 30 °C ) for 00:15:00 . Prepare DNase I stock solution by dissolving the lyophilized DNase I (1500 Kunitz units) in 550 µL RNase- free water. In some cases, the vial of DNase may appear to be empty. This is due to lyophilized enzyme sticking to the septum. To avoid loss of DNase I do not open the vial. Inject RNase-free water into the vial using an RNase-free needle and syringe. Mix gently by inverting the vial. Do not vortex. Prepare DNase I solution by adding 10 µL of DNase I stock solution into 70 µL of Buffer RDD. DNase I is especially sensitive to physical denaturation. Mixing should only be carried out by gently inverting the tube. Do not vortex.
Centrifuge at 8000 x g, Room temperature, 15 seconds . Discard the flow-through. Place the RNeasy MinElute spin column in a new collection tube.

Add 350 µL Buffer FRN to the RNeasy MinElute spin column. Close the lid gently. Before using Buffer FRN for the first time, check whether a precipitate has formed. If necessary, dissolve by warming with gentle agitation. After equilibration to room temperature ( 15 °C – 25 °C ), add 42 mL isopropanol (96–100%) to the entire concentrate (14 mL). Label the bottle.
Repeat the previous step until the entire sample has passed through the RNeasy MinElute spin column. Reuse the collection tube in the next step.
Centrifuge at 8000 x g, Room temperature, 15 seconds and discard the flow-through from the collection tube. Reuse the collection tube in the next step.
Transfer 700 µL of the sample to an RNeasy MinElute spin column placed in a 2 mL collection tube. Close the lid gently.
Add 1120 µL 100% ethanol and mix well by vortexing for 00:00:05 .
Add 320 µL of Buffer RLT to adjust binding conditions, and mix by vortexing,
Flash spin at Room temperature to remove drops from inside lid.

Add 500 µL Buffer FRN to the RNeasy MinElute spin column. Close the lid gently. Before using Buffer FRN for the first time, check whether a precipitate has formed. If necessary, dissolve by warming with gentle agitation. After equilibration to room temperature ( 15 °C 25 °C ), add 42 mL isopropanol (96–100%) to the entire concentrate (14 mL). Label the bottle.

Add 500 µL Buffer RPE to the RNeasy MinElute spin column. Close the lid gently. Add 4 volumes ( 44 mL ) ethanol (100% and molecular biology grade) to the bottle containing 11 mL Buffer RPE concentrate. Label the bottle. Before starting the procedure, mix reconstituted Buffer RPE by shaking.
Centrifuge at 8000 x g, Room temperature, 15 seconds . Discard the flow-through. Reuse the collection tube in the next step. RNA is bound to the RNeasy MinElute spin column membrane.
Place the same RNeasy MinElute spin column in a new 2 mL collection tube. Apply the flow-through from the previous step to the RNeasy MinElute spin column. Close the lid gently.
Centrifuge at 8000 x g, Room temperature, 15 seconds . Save the flow-through for use in the next step. Do not discard the flow-through, as it contains RNA including small RNAs.
Place the RNeasy MinElute spin column in a new 1.5 mL collection tube.
Centrifuge at 10000 x g, Room temperature, 2 minutes . Discard the collection tube with the flow-through.
Place the RNeasy MinElute spin column in the same collection tube.
Centrifuge at 8000 x g, Room temperature, 15 seconds to wash the spin column membrane. Discard the flow-through.
Add 500 µL Buffer RPE to the RNeasy MinElute spin column. Close the lid gently.
Centrifuge at 8000 x g, Room temperature, 15 seconds to wash the spin column membrane. Discard the flow-through. Reuse the collection tube in the next step.
Centrifuge at 10000 x g, Room temperature, 1 minute to elute the RNA. RNA is now in the flow- through, do not discard. RNA should be kept On ice for immediate use or stored long term at -80 °C .
Incubate for 00:02:00 at Room temperature .
Add 15 µL – 30 µL RNase-free water directly to the RNeasy MinElute spin column membrane.
Preserve the DNA pellet at -20 °C .

Measure the concentration and assess the quality of the extracted RNA using Nanodrop (Cytation 3 imaging reader and Gen5 Image 2.07 software). Ratio of 260/280 should be 1.8-2.0 and ratio of 260/230 is preferred to be 2.0-2.2.
